claim: Management materials continue to call out IC package substrate demand and capacity investment.
Japan electronics-materials trade data is a monitoring input for substrate bottlenecks.
Industry supply-chain mapping treats advanced substrates as a packaging constraint.
Investor materials report substrate demand as a main business driver.
Advanced packaging demand raises dependency on substrate and package assembly inputs.
Substrate concentration remains a supply-chain monitoring point.
ASE filings are the public anchor for advanced-packaging capacity monitoring.
3D packaging demand increases load on OSAT and package-test capacity.
Packaging capacity is a named semiconductor supply-chain constraint.
